text.title: Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol Worked Example
text.summary: One worked example tracing discovery-to-delivery-handover-protocol from trigger to validated artefact, including a deliberate failure-and-recovery cycle.

example.title: Outsource engagement, mid-sprint
example.code: Trigger: Discovery to Delivery Handover Protocol requested for engagement E-2026-014. Step 1 — applies_if check: - applies_if conditions reviewed against engagement: PASS Step 2 — prerequisites: - Collected per AGENTS.md prerequisites table. Step 3 — draft: - Applied rules from 01-core-rules.xml. - Populated output_payload with required fields. - Each rule_applied carries an evidence entry citing a source by url / ticket / doc. Step 4 — validate-output: - First pass FAILED: 'evidence array contains entry with source_type missing'. - Recovery: re-emitted with explicit source_type='url' + verifiable citation. - Second pass: VALID. Step 5 — commit: - Persisted to engagement log; consumer (Mariia, delivery PM) notified. - Owner (Oleh, BA) recorded; last_touched stamped.
example.note: The deliberate failure in step 4 is the most common observed failure mode (evidence-without-source). The recovery loop demonstrates the decision-gate behaviour pinned in content/04-procedure.xml.
